channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as huge parades and costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras in New Orleans and other southern cities.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hegseth orders suspension of Pentagon's offensive cyberoperations against Russia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has paused offensive cyberoperations against Russia by U.S. Cyber Command, rolling back some efforts to contend with a key adversary even as national security experts call for the U.S. to expand those capabilities. A U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Michigan mom seeks 'respect' for her 3 missing boys, asks a judge to declare them dead
item.description: ADRIAN, Mich. (AP) — The mother of three missing Michigan boys asked a judge Monday to declare them dead nearly 15 years later, saying they deserve the respect that they didn't get at the end of their young lives.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: The head of the FBI’s New York field office who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations has told coworkers that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Columbia takes disciplinary action months after anti-Israel agitators seized campus building
item.description: Columbia University is taking action against several students who were involved in an anti-Israel occupation of a campus building last spring at the New York City campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York City reports measles cases, raising public health concern
item.description: Measles is a highly contagious virus that can infect up to 90% of unvaccinated people who come in close proximity to an infected person, according to the New York City Department of Health.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers face second Musk deadline to explain their work last week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal employees face a midnight deadline to comply with Elon Musk 's second demand for reports on their recent accomplishments, a request that has become a flashpoint within the government workforce.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Education Department offers staff $25K incentive to quit before Monday deadline
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education is giving the majority of its staff a Monday deadline to accept a buyout of up to $25,000 ahead of reports of a 'very significant' reduction in force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human cannonball injured at Southern California county fair
item.description: Chachi "The Rocketman" Valencia was transported to a local hospital after he had fallen out of a safety net during the performance.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: With tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada now set to take effect on Tuesday, companies that depend on cross-border trade are already confronting increased costs.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Days after a judge sentenced a Virginia police officer to prison in the fatal shooting of an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses, the state's Republican governor granted the fired officer clemency, meaning he won't have to serve further time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says 25% tariffs on Mexican and Canadian imports will start Tuesday, with 'no room' for delay
item.description: President Donald Trump says 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada will start Tuesday, sparking renewed fears of a North American trade war.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, KRON4

item.title: Photos: A look at some of Oregon's worst invasive species
item.description: "Invasive species are a serious challenge for our parks and connected ecosystems,” the National Park Service website states.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, NewsNation

item.title: Ariana Grande goes full Glinda, 'Wicked' bestie Erivo does Elphaba proud in green on Oscars carpet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ariana Grande did her Glinda proud in a pale pink stunner, and her “Wicked” bestie Cynthia Erivo honored Elphaba in deep forest green on the Oscars carpet as high glam took over Sunday. Others went for bold red, classic black and pops of pink.
item.dc:creator: LEANNE ITALIE and BEATRICE DUPUY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 13 American universities slapped with ‘F’ grade on campus antisemitism
item.description: Thirteen universities across the United States were given "F" grades in the 2025 Campus Antisemitism Report Card released by the Anti-Defamation League.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chipotle will absorb tariff-related cost increases, CEO says
item.description: With the Trump administration's 25% tariff on all Mexican imports set to take effect Tuesday, Chipotle has pledged to absorb potential cost increases.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Today in history: 'Star-Spangled Banner' made official national anthem
item.description: It’s sung at athletic and political events alike — and Monday, "The Star-Spangled Banner” marks 94 years of being America’s national anthem.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: “This is a one time offer in advance of a very significant Reduction in Force for the US Department of Education,” Jacqueline Clay said.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cat food recalled due to bird flu risk
item.description: The FDA is alerting pet owners that some raw cat food is being recalled because it could be contaminated with bird flu.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Crews from across South Carolina help battle Carolina Forest-area wildfires
item.description: Regional and state fire rescue crews on Monday continued to battle the Carolina Forest-area wildfires, according to Horry County Fire Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, Fox 5

item.title: Oscars 2025: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, more left out of In Memoriam segment
item.description: As is nearly always the case, a few famous faces were missing from the In Memoriam segment — and their fans were quick to notice.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: In Memoriam snubs: Late actors who weren't honored at the Oscars
item.description: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, Tony Todd, and Quincy Jones were some of the notable figures not included in the In Memoriam segment at the 2024 Academy Awards, sparking outrage among their fans.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Kroger CEO abruptly resigns: What we know
item.description: Kroger's CEO, Rodney McMullen, has resigned due to an internal investigation into his personal conduct, unrelated to the business, and has been replaced by Ronald Sargent, who will serve as interim CEO and Chairman of the Board.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, KTLA 5

item.title: Waltz likens Zelenskyy to an 'ex-girlfriend' after Oval Office spat
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz compared Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to an “ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to watch when Trump gives his big speech to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loves a good spectacle, and it's hard to top a speech to a joint session of Congress. The House chamber is packed with lawmakers, and the president's arrival is announced in a booming voice by the sergeant-at-arms, triggering cacophonous applause.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida interstate ride-along offers rare inside look at illegal immigrant captures
item.description: Fox News Digital joined a Florida sheriff's office and federal immigration authorities on a ride-along as they carried out illegal immigration apprehensions along I-95.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: David Johansen, the wiry, gravelly-voiced singer and last surviving member of the glam and protopunk band the New York Dolls who later performed as his campy, pompadoured alter ego, Buster Poindexter, has died. He was 75.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, WFLA, NBC
